gazette: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Directorate for EU Affairs
title: Batteries and Waste Batteries Regulation adopted by the European Parliament and Council on July 10, 2023
bodyHtml:
The Council has adopted a new regulation strengthening sustainability rules for batteries and waste batteries. The regulation will regulate the entire life cycle of batteries, from production to reuse and recycling, ensuring their safety, sustainability, and competitiveness.

Addressing the full life cycle of batteries within the framework of circularity also requires regulations on how to treat batteries at the end of their lifespan, thereby supporting the creation of recycling markets and a market for secondary raw materials derived from waste batteries. Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is introduced for batteries that manufacturers introduce to the EU market for the first time. The new regulation aims to reduce environmental and social impacts throughout the lifespan of the battery. To this end, the regulation establishes strict rules for operators who must verify the source of the raw materials used for batteries placed on the market.

The regulation will replace the existing directive issued in 2006 and will complement the current legislation, particularly in terms of waste management.

For more detailed information:

Press release by the EU Council:

https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2023/07/10/council-adopts-new-regulation-on-batteries-and-waste-batteries/
